notes: Current golden GPIO baseline in default verification.
Known stable BMDA attach-load-attach-detach flash sequence.
Bench wired to ESP32-JTAG instrument; GPIO-only bench connections.

process_hardening.reason: Already established as a local baseline; less useful than F401 for a same-family F4 comparison with F411.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not broaden family coverage beyond existing F1 baseline.

targets.display_name: STM32F103 Bluepill GPIO Bench via ST-Link
notes: ST-Link variant of the stm32f103_gpio bench.
Exercises the local-managed ST-Link GDB server path.

process_hardening.reason: Instrument variant of the existing F1 baseline; adds no new family coverage.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not broaden family coverage.

notes: UART loopback bench variant of the STM32F103 Bluepill.
Exercises USART stimulus and capture paths via ESP32-JTAG.

process_hardening.reason: Bench wiring variant of existing F1 baseline; not a new target for pilot purposes.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not broaden family coverage.

notes: Larger F103 variant (RCT6) with more flash and RAM than the C8T6 Bluepill.
Bench wired to ESP32-JTAG instrument.

process_hardening.reason: Same STM32F1 family as existing baseline; adds marginal process coverage.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not broaden family coverage beyond existing F1.

notes: ST-Link instrument variant of the STM32F103RCT6 bench.
Exercises the local-managed ST-Link GDB server path on an RCT6 target.

process_hardening.reason: Instrument variant of RCT6; adds no new family coverage.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not broaden family coverage.

notes: Hardware-validated repeated golden GPIO baseline.
Closest current local baseline to stm32f411ceu6 in family and core class.
Still uses provisional AEL-owned startup/system code rather than official-source support.

process_hardening.reason: Lowest-risk second pilot because it shares the STM32F4 family with stm32f411ceu6 and already has repeated hardware validation.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not expand the process beyond the STM32F4 family.

notes: Draft placeholder for STM32F401CE bench configuration.
Bench connections not yet wired; placeholder pins only.

process_hardening.reason: Draft config only; not ready for pilot use.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not add new family coverage over existing F4 baselines.

notes: ST STM32F4 Discovery board via ST-Link instrument.
Cortex-M4 at 16 MHz; hardware verified in bench sessions.

process_hardening.reason: Same STM32F4 family as stm32f401rct6 primary candidate; less suitable.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not broaden family coverage beyond existing F4.

notes: ESP32-JTAG instrument variant of the STM32F4 Discovery bench.
Same target as stm32f407_discovery but exercises a different instrument path.

process_hardening.reason: Instrument variant of F407 Discovery; adds no new family coverage.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not broaden family coverage beyond existing F4.

notes: Draft placeholder for STM32F407VG bench configuration.
Bench connections not yet wired; placeholder pins only.

process_hardening.reason: Draft config only; not ready for pilot use.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not add new family coverage over existing F4 baselines.

notes: First official-source-based STM32 pilot in AEL.
Uses ST STM32CubeF4 startup/system/CMSIS support with AEL-owned main and linker script.
Board-level PC13 mapping remains provisional and plan-stage only.

process_hardening.reason: Already used as the first official-source pilot.

cross_family_proof.reason: Already covers the initial STM32F4 official-source proof.

notes: Current target is provisional and template-derived.
No hardware validation yet.
Strong candidate to prove the method on a non-F family while staying on Cortex-M4.

process_hardening.reason: Higher risk than F401 because it changes family and register support model at the same time.

cross_family_proof.reason: Best current candidate for broader proof because it moves from F4 to G4 without jumping to a much heavier MCU class.

notes: Draft placeholder for STM32G431CBU6 bench configuration.
Bench connections not yet wired; placeholder pins only.

process_hardening.reason: Draft config only; use stm32g431cbu6 for pilot planning.

cross_family_proof.reason: Draft config only; use stm32g431cbu6 for pilot planning.

notes: Current target is provisional and template-derived.
Cortex-M7 and broader device complexity make it a later pilot rather than the next one.

process_hardening.reason: Too large a jump for process hardening.

cross_family_proof.reason: Useful eventually, but less controlled than STM32G431 for the next family expansion.

notes: YD-STM32H750VBT6 board with ESP32-JTAG instrument.
Cortex-M7 at 64 MHz; higher complexity MCU class.
Build verified; bench connections require hardware validation.

process_hardening.reason: Too large a jump in MCU class for the immediate next process-hardening pilot.

cross_family_proof.reason: STM32H7 is a heavier jump than STM32G4 for the next family expansion.

notes: Current target is provisional and template-derived.
Moves to Cortex-M0 and a different low-level family model, so it is better after the method is hardened further.

process_hardening.reason: Too much architectural change for the immediate next pilot.

cross_family_proof.reason: Broadens family coverage, but M0 support is a sharper change than STM32G431.

notes: STM32F030C8T6 board via DAPLink instrument.
No hardware validation yet.

process_hardening.reason: No hardware validation yet; DAPLink instrument path not yet exercised.

cross_family_proof.reason: Same F0 family as existing stm32f030c8t6 baseline.

notes: STM32F030C8T6 UART bench variant via DAPLink.
No hardware validation yet.

cross_family_proof.reason: Same F0 family as existing stm32f030c8t6 baseline.

notes: Smaller STM32F103 variant (C6T6) with less flash than C8T6.

notes: STM32F401CE Black Pill board via ESP32JTAG.
Compact form factor variant of the STM32F4 family.

process_hardening.reason: Same F4 family as stm32f401rct6 primary candidate; adds marginal coverage.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not broaden family coverage beyond existing F4.

notes: STM32F407VET6 custom board via ESP32JTAG.
Cortex-M4 with FPU; multiple mailbox tests verified.

process_hardening.reason: Same F4 family as existing baselines.

cross_family_proof.reason: Does not broaden family coverage beyond existing F4.

notes: STM32H563RGT6 via DAPLink instrument.
Cortex-M33 with TrustZone; multiple mailbox tests across peripheral IPs verified.

process_hardening.reason: H5 complexity is higher than F4/G4; not suitable for near-term process hardening.

cross_family_proof.reason: Extends coverage to STM32H5 (Cortex-M33 + TrustZone) after G4 is proven.

notes: STM32U585CIU6 via ESP32JTAG.
Cortex-M33 ultra-low-power MCU with TrustZone; mailbox tests verified.

process_hardening.reason: U5 complexity and TrustZone add variables not needed for near-term hardening.

cross_family_proof.reason: Extends coverage to STM32U5 (Cortex-M33 + TrustZone) after G4 is proven.

notes: Variant of stm32f103_gpio_stlink using st-flash direct (no GDB/st-util).
First validation case for stlink_local instrument spec v1 backend.
Flash path goes through spec_executor -> st-flash binary.

process_hardening.reason: Instrument spec validation board; not a general hardening candidate.

cross_family_proof.reason: Same F1 family as existing baseline; not broadening family coverage.

why: Same STM32F4 family as the first official-source pilot.
Best target for proving the workflow can replace a known-good provisional/local baseline without changing too many variables.
Expands the method to STM32G4 rather than staying in STM32F4.
Keeps the jump smaller than STM32F756BGT6 by staying on Cortex-M4.
Better next-family proof candidate than F7 or F0 at the current maturity level.
